commonIssues:
Finding pre-independence or early post-independence records of grandparents/great-grandparents to prove caste lineage is extremely difficult, especially in rural areas.

How to fix:

  • Obtain old school records, Khatauni, or Khasra records from the Lekhpal or Tehsil office.
  • Request census records or village chaupal records from the District Archives.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit from two community elders along with their own caste certificates.
  • Apply via UP e-District Portal and mention document unavailability in remarks.

Applications pending at the SDM or Tehsil level for months due to backlog or inaction by local revenue staff.

How to fix:

  • Track status on UP e-District Portal using your application number.
  • File a grievance at Jansunwai UP Grievance Portal if delayed beyond 15 days.
  • Escalate to the District Magistrate (DM) office or attend the Jansunwai hearing.
  • Call the UP Government Helpline 1076 for status updates.

Frequent server errors, OTP failures, and document upload issues on the UP e-District portal.

How to fix:

  • Use Google Chrome (latest version) and clear browser cache before accessing the portal.
  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 100KB — compress using SmallPDF.
  • Try during off-peak hours (early morning).
  • If OTP fails, ensure your mobile number matches Aadhaar and retry after 5 minutes.
  • Visit the nearest Jan Seva Kendra / CSC for assisted filing.

Revenue Inspector (RI) or Lekhpal not visiting for field verification, or demanding bribes for completing verification.

How to fix:

  • Keep all original documents ready at home during the enquiry period.
  • If officer doesn't visit, file a complaint at Jansunwai Portal.
  • If facing bribery demands, report to the UP Anti-Corruption Helpline 1064.
  • Request a written acknowledgement of your complaint for follow-up.

Errors in the issued certificate such as wrong caste name, misspelled names, or incorrect father's name.

How to fix:

  • Apply for certificate correction at the Tehsil office with the original certificate and supporting documents.
  • Submit Aadhaar, school records, and family's existing certificates as evidence.
  • If correction is refused, escalate via Jansunwai Portal.
  • For online certificates, request re-issuance through e-District Portal.

Applications rejected without providing a clear written reason, leaving applicants confused about next steps.

How to fix:

  • Request a written reason for rejection from the Tehsildar — this is your right.
  • File an appeal with the Additional District Magistrate (ADM) within 30 days.
  • Lodge a grievance at Jansunwai or call 1076.
  • Seek free legal aid from the DLSA — NALSA Portal.

OBC applicants face additional hurdles in obtaining the Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) certificate along with the caste certificate.

How to fix:

  • Apply for NCL certificate separately via e-District Portal.
  • Provide 3-year Income Tax Returns of both parents as income proof.
  • If parents are government employees, submit salary slips and Form-16.
  • Current NCL limit is ₹8 lakhs per annum — check latest DoPT notification.

Unofficial agents/touts exploit applicants by charging ₹500-₹2000 for a free service, especially in rural areas.

How to fix:

  • Apply directly online via UP e-District Portal — the service is free.
  • Visit an authorized Jan Seva Kendra / CSC where nominal charges apply (₹10-₹30).
  • Report agents to the DM office or Jansunwai Portal.
  • Never share your Aadhaar OTP or login credentials with unauthorized persons.
